description: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet revealed her father's advice after losing a speech contest shaped her career: 'You have to be so much better than everyone else, that they must pick you.' Bourla echoed this sentiment, emphasizing the danger of aiming too low.

description: The protest attracted hundreds of participants, most of them students and young professionals. Many attendees wore cockroach masks and carried flowers, while schoolchildren were also seen at the venue accompanied by their parents. Protesters raised slogans and called for the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over issues linked to examinations such as NEET, CBSE, CUET and SSC.

Bill proposes ending H-1B path to permanent residency and eliminating OPT program
description: U.S. Representative Chip Roy has introduced the "American White-Collar Worker Jobs Act of 2026" to reform the H-1B visa program. The bill aims to end H-1B visas as a path to permanent residency and eliminate the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program. It seeks to prioritize American STEM professionals by enforcing stricter wage standards and preventing displacement of U.S. workers.

description: Hauz Rani grapples with the aftermath of a fatal fire, highlighting the pervasive danger of tangled overhead wires and inadequate infrastructure. Residents voice concerns about authorities only acting after tragedies, leaving them to rely on themselves in emergencies. The constant threat of electrical hazards is a grim reality locals have learned to endure.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee’s 'health message' to Anthropic, OpenAI & Google
description: Meta's top AI executive, Alexandr Wang, revealed the company's strategy to challenge rivals like OpenAI and Google by focusing on health-related AI capabilities. While acknowledging current models aren't top-tier, Wang highlighted Meta's commitment to advancing AI for health applications, aiming to integrate these features into popular platforms like Facebook and Instagram.
